stem: Which of the following expressions has a factor of x + 3b, where b is a positive integer constant?
rationale: If x + 3b is a factor of an expression 2x² + cx + 21b, the other factor must be 2x + 7 since the leading coefficient is 2 and 3b · 7 = 21b. Multiplying: (x + 3b)(2x + 7) = 2x² + (7 + 6b)x + 21b. So 7 + 6b must equal the coefficient of x in the expression, and b must be a positive integer. Of the four choices, only 49 yields an integer value: 7 + 6b = 49 gives b = 7.
